title: Pointing things out & giving examples
why_grouped_en: These five all help you single something out or give an example — but learners mix them up. "For example" and "such as" introduce examples; "especially", "particularly" and "in particular" stress that one thing stands out from the rest.
why_grouped_mm: ဥပမာ ပေးတဲ့အခါ၊ တစ်ခုကို အထူးပြုပြောတဲ့အခါ သုံးတဲ့ စကားလုံးတွေ။

words.short_en: here is one case that shows my point
words.short_mm: ဥပမာအားဖြင့်
words.explanation_en: Introduces a full example, often a whole clause. Frequently starts a sentence, set off with a comma. Often shortened to "e.g." in writing.
words.nuance_en: Followed by an example sentence or noun. "For example, prices have risen sharply."

examples.en: Some animals hibernate. Bears, for example, sleep all winter.
You could save money — for example, by cooking at home.

words.explanation_en: Introduces examples inside a sentence — usually a list of nouns, not a full clause. No comma before it when the examples are essential.
words.nuance_en: Use it mid-sentence with nouns: "fruits such as mangoes and bananas". Don't follow it with a full sentence.

examples.en: I enjoy team sports such as football and basketball.
Bring warm clothes such as a jacket and gloves.

words.short_mm: အထူးသဖြင့်
words.explanation_en: Singles out one thing as the most important or extreme in a group. Often after a comma.
words.nuance_en: Don't confuse with "specially" (= for a special purpose). "I love fruit, especially mangoes."

examples.en: It gets cold here, especially at night.

words.explanation_en: Very close to "especially". Also means "very" in the negative: "not particularly good" = not very good.
words.nuance_en: Slightly more formal than "especially". "The exam wasn't particularly hard."

examples.en: Traffic is bad, particularly on Mondays.
I'm not particularly hungry right now.

words.short_mm: အထူးအားဖြင့်
words.explanation_en: Points to one specific item, often placed after the noun it marks. "One dish in particular stood out."
words.nuance_en: Comes after the thing it highlights, unlike "especially" which comes before. "Was there anything in particular you wanted?"

examples.en: I liked the music, one song in particular.
Is there anyone in particular you'd like to invite?

exercises.prompt: Mid-sentence list of nouns: "I read fiction ___ thrillers and mysteries."
Starting a sentence with a full example: "___, many people now work from home."
Singling one thing out before the noun: "I love the summer, ___ the long evenings."
After the noun it highlights: "Was there a colour ___ you had in mind?"
